column3: description

column1: # Void-touched scholar. Inquisitor, archivist, voice of the first. Orthodoxy-adjacent, dangerous.
# === L1 Core fill (2) — mobility/utility/reaction to round out the roster ===

column3: Step between the pages of what is and what was. Short RESONANCE-guided teleport; you briefly cease to be witnessed, breaking line of effect on enemies you pass.

Every sutra has an unwritten edge. Make a VEIL check on one enemy; you learn one truth about them — a weakness, a fear, or what they refuse to say — and your next action against them hits as if it were already sentenced.
You have begun writing in the sacred spaces. Once per encounter, rewrite one die you just rolled on a RESONANCE or VEIL check; the correction becomes canon.
Inhale the stillness between prayers. Regain 1 AP and clear one minor status on yourself; you may not speak (cannot use cards with a spoken effect) until your next turn.

column1: # === L3 Inquisitor (4) — hunts apostates, void-blessed interrogator ===

column3: Point, name the sin, and the void remembers. Apply the Apostate mark to one enemy for the encounter; your strikes against Apostates deal extra VEIL damage and ignore resonance cover.
Drag one enemy into the thin place where lies cannot hold. Target makes a VEIL save; on failure, they reveal one card in hand or one tactic — you gain a counter to it.
The guilty confess whether they mean to or not. RESONANCE strike against an Apostate; if it hits, the target loses 1 AP on their next turn as the brand burns.
The void pulls the faithless home. Drag one Apostate adjacent to you; they cannot move further from you than short range until end of your next turn.

column1: # === L3 Archive Master (4) — keeper of forbidden sutensai knowledge ===

column3: Unshelve a ruling no one has read in a century. Draw two cards; if either is a subclass card, reduce its AP cost by 1 the first time you play it this encounter.
Cite a sutra the Priors forbade. VEIL attack that uses an enemy's own resonance against them; the damage scales with their resonance score, not yours.
Append a correction to the world. Choose one recent event this round (a hit, a miss, a movement); you may re-read it as if it happened slightly differently. Once per encounter.

Every enemy has a filing. Spend a short action to index one enemy; you and allies gain advantage on the next check made against them, and you may recall that index later to cast it again as a free card.

column1: # === L3 Priors Voice (4) — channels the first sutensai, historic authority ===

column3: Speak in the voice that named the void. One enemy must pass a RESONANCE save or stand transfixed until the start of your next turn; allies who hear you gain courage and shed one fear condition.
You are not speaking — the First is speaking through you. Your next card played this turn is treated as if cast by a Prior: ignore resistance, and treat failed saves against it as critical.
The long-dead rebuke the living. Reaction: when an enemy hits an ally, speak the rebuke; the attacker takes RESONANCE damage and has disadvantage on their next attack.
Call the first sutensai to watch this moment. For the rest of your turn, every card you play also inflicts a minor VEIL tremor on its target; at turn's end, you take 1 HP as the Witness withdraws.

column1: # === L5 Power (2 more) — Channel Prior already exists ===

column3: Recite the sutra that undoes what has been recorded. Choose one enemy buff, summoned creature, or terrain-inscription in play; it ceases to have been. The target may contest with a RESONANCE save.

Open a seam of the void and let the hush pour through. Area effect: all enemies in a short-range zone lose the ability to use spoken or resonance-powered abilities until the start of your next turn; allies in the zone regain 1 AP.
You are court, jury, and void-blessed blade. Make three RESONANCE strikes in sequence against one Apostate; between strikes, the target must answer (RESONANCE save) or shed one AP per failed answer.

Pronounce verdict in the old tongue. One Apostate you can see must pass a RESONANCE+VEIL save; on failure, they are removed from the field until start of your next turn, standing in a place that is not quite here.

You crack the seal the Priors swore you would not. Draw four cards; any of them marked forbidden may be played this encounter ignoring their normal restrictions. At end of encounter, one random card is lost from your deck forever.

Some names should not be in the record. Choose one enemy; until end of encounter, references to them are struck from the field — they have disadvantage on all attacks against you, and allies treat them as unseen until the first time they strike.

The First sutensai speaks with your mouth. Until your next turn, your Priors Voice cards cost 1 less AP (minimum 1) and inflict VEIL fear on the first target each round. You cannot speak in any other tongue.

Summon the echo of every Prior who ever held this office. Each ally gains a +1 to their next RESONANCE or VEIL roll this encounter; each enemy in sight must pass a RESONANCE save or kneel, losing their next action.
